1.description: The binomial tree under theR$\mathbb {R}$measure. This figure shows a two-year binomial tree structure for the stock price and the expected call price under theR$\mathbb {R}$measure. TheR$\mathbb {R}$measure follows a physicalP$\mathbb {P}$distribution until beforeH=1$H=1$years, and a risk-neutralQ$\mathbb {Q}$distribution beginning at timeH=1$H=1$years. The following parameters are used:St=100$S_t=100$,K=100$K=100$,μ=0.1$\mu =0.1$,r=0.03$r=0.03$,σ=0.15$\sigma =0.15$, andΔt=1$\Delta t =1$. The up and down node values of the future stock price evolution are computed using the binomial-tree method given by Cox, Ross, and Rubinstein (1979). The up and downR$\mathbb {R}$probabilities are the same as the corresponding up and downP$\mathbb {P}$probabilities at time 0 equal to 0.812 and 0.188, respectively, and the up and downR$\mathbb {R}$probabilities are the same as the corresponding up and downQ$\mathbb {Q}$probabilities at time 1 equal to 0.564 and 0.436, respectively. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]
